metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the preferred outcome in AC2 — either 'delete the helper outright' or 'leave a deprecation stub (with rationale and a removal ticket)' — so the executor has an unambiguous target.
findings.finding: AC2 presents two meaningfully different end-states ('deleted' vs 'reduced to a deprecation stub') without stating which is preferred, leaving the executor to make an architectural choice the plan author should decide.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC2: 'The `_load` helper is deleted (or reduced to a deprecation stub with no callers)'
findings.suggested_fix: Preferred: 'The `_load` helper is deleted with no remaining callers.' If a stub is ever warranted, add a rationale and reference a follow-up ticket.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define 'optionality guard' in AC5 (e.g. the specific test, script, or import path that embodies it) and supply the concrete command that proves it is still green.
findings.finding: AC5's phrase 'the optionality guard stays green' uses unexplained internal jargon with no proving command, so an executor cannot verify this criterion without out-of-band knowledge.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC5: '`python -c "import rebar._engine.rebar_reconciler"` and the optionality guard stay green'
findings.suggested_fix: E.g. 'the optional-dependency guard (python -c "import rebar._engine.rebar_reconciler" with optional dep X absent) exits 0' — or replace with the actual test name/command.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Harden AC4 with a concrete proving command, e.g. name one specific converted symbol and provide a `pyright --outputjson` invocation (or equivalent) whose output confirms zero 'cannot resolve import' errors for the converted modules.
findings.finding: AC4's Pyright/Serena spot-check has no concrete proving command — no named symbol, no tool invocation, no expected output — making it non-reproducible across executors.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC4: 'Pyright/Serena can resolve references into the converted modules (spot-check: find-references on a converted symbol returns its call sites)'
findings.suggested_fix: E.g. 'pyright src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/ --outputjson | jq -e \'[.generalDiagnostics[] | select(.rule=="reportMissingImports")] | length == 0\'' — run before and after to confirm the conversion eliminates all prior resolution failures.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify and document whether the test tree's pervasive `spec_from_file_location` loading convention (and sys.modules key pre-seeding for stub injection) must be migrated alongside the production `_load()` calls, or add a compatibility strategy — the current plan scopes only production call sites but the test infrastructure depends on the same by-path loading mechanism.
findings.finding: The plan asserts the path-based _load() mechanism 'is now pure legacy' but _loader.py's own docstring explicitly states the mechanism remains necessary because 'the top-level rebar_reconciler name is shadowed by a same-named test package' and modules are 'exec'd standalone via spec_from_file_location (no package context)'. The conftest.py and ~100 test files confirm this: the entire reconciler test tree loads modules via spec_from_file_location and pre-seeds sys.modules with exact string keys for stub injection — a pattern that is load-bearing and incompatible with plain `from . import` without a parallel migration of the test infrastructure.
findings.location: Why section / Scope section ('pure legacy' claim); _loader.py lines 1-29; tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/conftest.py lines 1-88
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sub-task: 'Audit test loader compatibility — assess whether converting production modules to package imports breaks the spec_from_file_location + sys.modules stub-injection pattern used in ~100 reconciler tests, and define the migration path (e.g., keep the _loader.py helper for tests, or convert test loaders in the same PR).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `__main__.py`'s `_load_sibling_keyed()` (and its test mock in `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/orchestrate/test_main_entry.py`) to the explicit edit-target list, or consciously exclude it with rationale.
findings.finding: `__main__.py` contains its own independent path-based loader `_load_sibling_keyed()` (lines 49–70, called at lines 516 and 534 for `mode.py` and `_advisory_lock.py`) that is not `lazy_load`/`_load()` and is not enumerated anywhere in the plan as an edit target, yet it is a path-based sibling loader inside the plan's stated scope directory.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Scope section: 'Also covers `__main__._load_sibling_keyed` (the parallel path-based loader for `mode.py` and `_advisory_lock.py`); `test_main_entry.py`'s `patch.object(main_mod, "_load_sibling_keyed", ...)` must be updated if `_load_sibling_keyed` is converted or removed.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a scope note explaining how the `sys.modules` key contract will be migrated (either update all pre-seeding test sites to use the new canonical keys, or confirm that `monkeypatch`/`mock.patch` on dotted module paths handles this automatically), and remove the 'pass unchanged' claim from AC3 if test updates are required.
findings.finding: The plan does not enumerate that the test infrastructure across `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/` (100+ test files) uses a pervasive `sys.modules` pre-seeding pattern that is tightly coupled to the CURRENT flat string keys used by `lazy_load` (e.g. `"reconcile_config"`, `"rebar_reconciler_errors"`, `"rebar_reconciler.alert_store"`). Converting to package imports changes how production code registers modules in `sys.modules` — the old flat keys would no longer match what production modules look up, breaking test stub injection. The `_loader.py` docstring explicitly calls this out as 'load-bearing'.
findings.location: Scope section / AC3 ('reconciler test suites pass unchanged')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the line reference in the Why section from `reconcile.py:697` to `reconcile.py:587` (or use the `_load()` definition at line 258).
findings.finding: The plan cites `reconcile.py:697` as the primary example `_load()` call site, but the actual `_load("reconcile_run_differs", "run_differs.py")` call is at line 587 in the current file; line 697 is inside a `_PassContext` field assignment block. The reference is stale/inaccurate.
findings.location: Why section — 'reconcile.py:697 (and the reconciler's `_loader` helper) loads sibling modules by file path'
findings.suggested_fix: Change 'reconcile.py:697' to 'reconcile.py:587' (the `_load("reconcile_run_differs", "run_differs.py")` call) or 'reconcile.py:258' (the `_load` function definition).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how module-level ordinary/relative imports in converted modules will coexist with the pervasive `spec_from_file_location` test-loader pattern (150+ sites in `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/`) that loads engine modules without package context — either by migrating those loaders to normal imports, or by establishing that function-local lazy imports replace ALL module-level `_load()` uses, so no module-level relative import is added.
findings.finding: The plan's proposed mechanism (replace `_load()` with ordinary `from . import` / package imports) does not account for the 'no package context' constraint that is the documented root cause of the current pattern. Modules loaded via `spec_from_file_location` without a package context (both in the 200+ test call sites and in `__main__.py`'s `_try_load_step`) cannot resolve relative imports (`from . import x`) at module-execution time. The existing try/except bootstrap pattern in `run_differs.py` and `reconcile.py` exists precisely for this reason. After conversion, any module with a module-level `from . import mutation` will fail when loaded via `spec_from_file_location` in the test suite, unless every test call site is also migrated to normal imports or the package context is otherwise established — a dependency the plan does not mention.
findings.location: ## What / ## Acceptance Criteria / _loader.py docstring lines 3-10 / tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/conftest.py lines 7-28
findings.suggested_fix: Clarify in 'What' that ALL converted `_load()` call sites must use function-local (deferred) `from rebar_reconciler import x` or `import rebar_reconciler.x` imports (never module-level), since these are only evaluated when the enclosing function runs and package context is available via sys.modules by that point. This preserves the existing test-loader pattern. Alternatively, specify that the test-suite loader pattern is also converted to use `importlib.import_module('rebar._engine.rebar_reconciler.x')` (establishes package context), but call out this is a substantial parallel effort.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the scope and AC grep to cover ALL path-based loading patterns in the package, not just `_load(` calls — specifically the inline `spec_from_file_location` calls in `pass_io.py`, `binding_store.py`, `inbound_translate.py`, and the `_load_sibling_keyed`/`_try_load_step` helpers in `__main__.py`, or explicitly justify excluding them.
findings.finding: The plan's scope and AC grep target (`_load(` under `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/`) will miss several other path-based loading sites in the same package that are NOT routed through the named `_load()` helper: `pass_io.py` (two inline `spec_from_file_location` calls at lines 72-79 and 90-95), `binding_store.py` (inline `spec_from_file_location` at line 224), `inbound_translate.py` (inline call at line 138), and `__main__.py`'s `_load_sibling_keyed` / `_try_load_step` helpers. These leave the static-analysis opacity problem partially unsolved.
findings.location: ## Acceptance Criteria – AC1 / pass_io.py:72-95 / binding_store.py:224 / inbound_translate.py:138 / __main__.py:49-103

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify (and document in the plan) how the flat `sys.modules` key contract used by >150 reconciler test files (e.g. `"reconcile_applier"`, `"reconcile_mutation"`, `"rebar_reconciler_errors"`) is migrated when production code switches to dotted package imports — either by updating all test pre-seedings to the new canonical keys, or by confirming that `monkeypatch`/`unittest.mock.patch` targets will resolve correctly against the new import paths.
findings.finding: The plan asserts that replacing `_load()` with `from . import …` / `import rebar._engine.rebar_reconciler.…` is straightforwardly achievable, but does not verify how the test harness's load-bearing `sys.modules`-key contract is migrated. The existing flat keys (e.g. `"reconcile_applier"`, `"reconcile_mutation"`, `"rebar_reconciler_errors"`) pre-seeded by >150 test files will no longer match the canonical dotted keys that ordinary package imports register (e.g. `"rebar._engine.rebar_reconciler.applier"`), silently breaking `patch()` targets and test stub injection.
findings.location: What / Acceptance Criteria (AC: `make lint && make typecheck && make test` green; reconciler test suites pass unchanged)
findings.suggested_fix: Add an explicit migration note to the plan's Scope section: the sys.modules-seeding pattern in test fixtures must be updated in lock-step with each converted module (old flat key → new canonical dotted key, e.g. `"reconcile_applier"` → `"rebar._engine.rebar_reconciler.applier"`), and the conftest's `__path__`-extension workaround must be assessed for removal. Consider adding an AC that runs a grep for the old flat keys against test files to confirm full migration.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that `python -c "import rebar._engine.rebar_reconciler"` actually works in the installed package (check whether rebar/_engine/__init__.py exists) and confirm this tests the real deployment import path; if the reconciler is only ever loaded as top-level `rebar_reconciler`, use that form instead.
findings.finding: AC5 specifies `python -c "import rebar._engine.rebar_reconciler"` as the import-cycle verification command, but the reconciler is designed and deployed as the top-level package `rebar_reconciler` (launched via `python -m rebar_reconciler` with `_engine` on sys.path, per __main__.py lines 30-40 and __init__.py). The __main__.py bootstrap even tries `import rebar` to discover the parent, not as a subpackage. Whether `rebar._engine.rebar_reconciler` resolves identically as a package import test is unverified — the package may not be registered under that dotted path in a normal install.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC5: 'python -c "import rebar._engine.rebar_reconciler" and the optionality guard stay green'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct or remove the 'import-linter layering checks' claim in the Why section — no import-linter tool or configuration exists in the repo; the optionality CI job by that name runs AST-based pytest tests, not the import-linter package.
findings.finding: The plan cites 'import-linter layering checks' as a static tool the repo relies on that is defeated by path-based loading. No import-linter configuration file (.importlinter, setup.cfg [importlinter], or pyproject.toml [tool.importlinter]) exists in the repo. The 'import-linter' job in optionality.yml runs pytest AST tests (test_core_optionality.py), not the import-linter pip package. The claim that path-based loading 'defeats import-linter layering checks' is unsupported.
findings.location: Why section: 'defeats every static tool the repo relies on — Pyright/Serena reference resolution, import-linter layering checks'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'import-linter layering checks' with 'the AST-based optionality guard (test_core_optionality.py)' or simply remove it, since that guard targets heavy-stack imports, not layering between reconciler modules.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a positive rationale in 'What' or 'Why' that explains which import form is appropriate (e.g., function-local `from rebar_reconciler import x` works under both the top-level and subpackage identity because it executes after sys.modules is populated, whereas module-level relative imports fail when loaded via spec_from_file_location) so the implementer can make correct local decisions.
findings.finding: The plan states a rationale for WHAT the change achieves (re-enabling static tools) but provides no positive rationale for WHY ordinary package imports are viable under the actual deployment constraints — specifically the dual-identity loading (package is both `rebar._engine.rebar_reconciler` as a subpackage and `rebar_reconciler` as a top-level module). Without this, the implementer has no guidance on which import form to use (`from . import x` vs `import rebar._engine.rebar_reconciler.x` vs `from rebar_reconciler import x`) and under what invocation each form works.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Align all references to the helper function under a single name (`_load` or `_loader`) across Why, What, Scope, and AC2 so the grep target is unambiguous.
findings.finding: Terminology drift between 'Why' and 'What'/'ACs': the Why section calls the target artifact the '_loader helper' while the What section and all ACs call it '_load()' / '_load helper'. An implementer scanning the codebase for the helper to delete could be confused about whether the function is named `_load` or `_loader`.
findings.location: Why section ('the reconciler's `_loader` helper') vs What section ('delete the _load() helper') and AC2 ('_load helper is deleted')
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'the reconciler's `_loader` helper' in the Why section with 'the reconciler's `_load()` helper' to match the AC and What sections.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Remove the '(or reduced to a deprecation stub with no callers)' escape hatch from AC2, or add a corresponding description in the What section that justifies when a stub is acceptable and what it must contain.
findings.finding: AC2 weakens the stated deletion goal by allowing 'a deprecation stub with no callers' as an alternative to full deletion. The What section says 'delete the _load() helper once callerless' with no mention of a stub; the AC's stub escape hatch leaves open the possibility of dead code remaining, undermining the audit finding that motivated the story.
findings.location: AC2: 'The `_load` helper is deleted (or reduced to a deprecation stub with no callers)'
findings.suggested_fix: If the intent is a clean deletion, rewrite AC2 as: 'The `_load` helper function is fully removed from the codebase (grep-verifiable: zero definitions of `def _load` remain under `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/`).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the scope (or explicitly OUT-scope with rationale) to address the independent spec_from_file_location sibling-loading patterns in pass_io.py, binding_store.py, inbound_translate.py, differ.py, and __main__.py — these are the same mechanism as _load() but are not captured by the 'all _load() call sites' scope.
findings.finding: The plan scopes the conversion to 'all _load() call sites under src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/' but several modules in that same directory use independent spec_from_file_location patterns that are not routed through _load()/_loader.py: pass_io.py (lines 72-79, 90-94 — loads conflict_resolver and alert_store directly), binding_store.py (line 224 — loads alert_store), inbound_translate.py (line 138 — loads config), differ.py (lines 33, 65-73 — has its own spec_from_file_location loop), and __main__.py (_load_sibling_keyed and _try_load_step helpers). After deleting _load()/lazy_load(), these independent paths remain.
findings.location: Scope section: 'In: all _load() call sites under src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or expand AC1/AC2) to confirm zero `_load(` call sites exist **repo-wide** (not just under `rebar_reconciler/`) before the helper is deleted, or explicitly assert that `_load` is not importable/callable from outside `rebar_reconciler/`.
findings.finding: AC1 scopes the 'zero _load( call sites' grep to `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/` only, but AC2 deletes the `_load` helper entirely. If `_load` is imported or called from anywhere else in the repo (e.g., other engine modules or tests outside that directory), those callers are silently broken and are not acknowledged.
findings.suggested_fix: Expand AC1 to: 'Zero path-based `_load(` call sites remain anywhere in the repo (grep-verifiable: `grep -r "_load(" src/ tests/`)'; or add a note in scope confirming `_load` is a private helper with no external callers.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Broaden AC1's grep criterion to cover both `_load(` and `lazy_load(` (and imports of `_loader`), or restate it as 'Zero `lazy_load(` / `_load(` call sites and no `from rebar_reconciler._loader import` remain under the target directory'.
findings.finding: The plan describes the shared helper as '`_load()` helper' and targets '`_load(` call sites', but the actual shared centralised mechanism is `lazy_load()` in `_loader.py`, imported by 13 sibling modules — the local `_load()` wrappers in `reconcile.py` and `run_differs.py` are thin delegators. Only those 2 files define a local `_load()` function; 11 more files call `lazy_load()` directly. The AC's grep string '`_load(`' will NOT catch direct `lazy_load(` call sites in `outbound_differ.py`, `apply_planning.py`, `outbound_fields.py`, `outbound_comments.py`, `inbound_translate.py`, `rebar_id_audit.py`, `reconcile_check.py`, `_ref_lock.py`, `apply_base.py`, `invariants.py`, `_advisory_lock.py`.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace AC1 with: 'Zero `lazy_load(`/`_load(` call sites and no `from rebar_reconciler._loader import` lines remain under `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/` (grep: `lazy_load\|from rebar_reconciler._loader\|_load(`)'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
